subject: Computer Networking
title: Power over Ethernet (PoE)
description: A quick, practical guide to Power over Ethernet (PoE).

hook.text: What if one cable delivered both data and power? That's Power over Ethernet.

buildup.text: PoE sends DC power over the same copper pairs that carry Ethernet data. Devices extract what they need.

discovery.text: PoE powers IP cameras, Wi-Fi access points, VoIP phones, and IoT sensors. No separate outlet needed.

twist.text: PoE+ delivers 30 watts. PoE++ delivers 90 — enough for monitors, thin clients, and some laptops.

climax.text: A PoE switch powers a whole floor of APs from the server closet. One cable per device simplifies everything.

punchline.text: One cable, two jobs: data and power. PoE simplifies everything.

quiz.question: How much power can PoE++ deliver per port?
